properties.tree_description:
None Reason for Removal:OtherComments:The tree exhibits more than 30% canopy dieback, with a thin overall crown and visible tip dieback. Several scaffold branches are dead, contributing to reduced canopy density. A basal cavity is present, with visible internal decay and associated wood loss confirmed through zoom inspection. A vertical crease with an associated wound is observed along the trunk on the west side, indicating localized structural compromise at the base.
		
		
			Tree number:2Species:Water Oak Tree Size (DBH):2Tree location:RearDescription of Tree:None Reason for Removal:OtherComments:The tree is heavily infested with mature wisteria, with significant vine growth concentrated on the east side and extending throughout the canopy. The vine has developed substantial caliper and occupies much of the interior structure, contributing to the displacement of functional foliage to the outer canopy. The remaining live growth is largely confined to distal branch tips, resulting in a lion-tailed structure with reduced interior canopy density.

A Pecan on the rear of home, with a DBH of 20.5in. There is hollow space in the trunk, approximately3-4ft long.Reason for Removal:Diseased
